company_snapshot.name: DevCo
company_snapshot.description: DevCo provides a pipeline observability and CI/CD intelligence platform that gives engineering teams visibility into build flakiness, test failures, and deployment bottlenecks across GitHub Actions, CircleCI, and Jenkins.

triggers.event: Raised $28M Series B led by Bessemer Venture Partners on February 19, 2025. Press release highlights plan to expand into enterprise segment and hire 40+ engineers in H1 2025.
triggers.sales_relevance: Series B + stated headcount growth plan = immediate infrastructure and tooling spend. Enterprise expansion means they will be evaluating more mature DevOps tooling. High probability of active vendor evaluations in the next 60-90 days.

triggers.event: Marcus Webb joined as VP Engineering on March 10, 2025, previously Head of Platform at Stripe. Announcement published on DevCo engineering blog.
triggers.sales_relevance: New VP Engineering from Stripe's platform team will have high standards for developer tooling and will likely audit the current stack. He is exactly the right champion persona — platform-focused, experienced with scale. First 60 days is the evaluation window.

triggers.event: Posted 9 open SRE and Platform Engineering roles on LinkedIn in February-March 2025, several requiring experience with Datadog, OpenTelemetry, and Kubernetes.
triggers.sales_relevance: Hiring SREs and platform engineers at this scale signals they are building out an internal platform team — the exact buyer persona. These hires will be evaluating on-call and observability tooling as part of their mandate.

triggers.event: Published case study on March 5, 2025 featuring Ironhaven Labs as a customer, describing a 60% reduction in flaky test rate. Shared widely on LinkedIn and Hacker News (238 upvotes).
triggers.sales_relevance: Strong social proof signal — content marketing at this volume suggests an active GTM motion and growing inbound. Not a direct sales trigger, but useful for personalizing outreach: 'saw your Ironhaven case study on HN...'

notable_hiring_areas: Sales (7 open roles, including enterprise AEs)
growth_indicators.geographic_expansion: No new office announced; continuing remote-first policy; new enterprise sales focus likely targeting large US tech companies

output.recommended_angle: Lead with Marcus Webb's hire and connect to the platform engineering scale-up. He joined 19 days ago from Stripe's platform team — he will be assessing every tool in the stack. The angle: 'Marcus, congrats on joining DevCo — with the Series B fresh and a mandate to hire 40 engineers, the on-call and incident management complexity is about to spike. At Stripe, you were running one of the world's most demanding platform environments. Curious if DevCo's current incident tooling will scale with you, or if you're evaluating options. Happy to share what the SRE team at [Reference Customer] did in the same situation.'
output.raw_research_notes: Datadog confirmed via multiple job postings requiring 'Datadog experience' and a company engineering blog post referencing their observability stack. PagerDuty confirmed via a job posting for an SRE role listing 'PagerDuty administration' as a requirement — interesting because this means they are using the product we may be able to displace or augment. Linear confirmed via a tweet from their Head of Engineering in January 2025. Greenhouse confirmed via direct application links on careers page. No negative signals found. CEO Priya Nair has been active on LinkedIn — 3 posts in the past 2 weeks about the Series B and engineering culture. Company appears very healthy. High-priority prospect.
